subject: Cloud Computing
title: Client-Server: The Desktop Revolution
description: A short lesson to help you apply Client-Server: The Desktop Revolution.

hook.text: By the 1990s, every office had PCs wired to a central server down the hall.

buildup.text: The client-server model split work: desktops handled the interface, servers stored data.

discovery.text: Companies built server rooms, hired IT teams, and managed all their own hardware.

twist.text: It worked until it didn't. Servers crashed, backups failed, and scaling was a nightmare.

climax.text: Managing your own servers was expensive and risky. Companies wanted a better way.

punchline.text: Everyone wanted servers. Nobody wanted to manage them.

quiz.question: What was the main drawback of the client-server model for businesses?
options: Companies had to buy, maintain, and scale their own hardware
Client-server networks could not connect to the internet
Only government agencies were allowed to use servers
